content_markdown: What does the constant $n$ represent in the equation of state for an ideal gas $pV = nRT$?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: The number of atoms in the gas

options.label: B
options.text: The number of moles of the gas

options.label: C
options.text: The number of molecules of the gas

options.label: D
options.text: The number of particles in the gas

vibe_explanation: In the ideal gas law equation $pV = nRT$, $n$ specifically refers to the amount of substance in moles. If the equation used the Boltzmann constant $k_B$ (i.e., $pV = N k_B T$), then $N$ would represent the number of molecules or particles.
